user_stories.goal: Generate a personalised day-by-day city itinerary from the web app by entering destination, trip length, budget, interests and travel style
user_stories.benefit: Get a clear, ready-to-follow plan that matches my time, budget and interests
acceptance_criteria: The Flask API exposes GET /health that returns 200 and a basic JSON status
The Flask API exposes POST /api/itinerary and accepts JSON payload with keys: destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style
A successful POST /api/itinerary response returns HTTP 200 and a JSON object containing top-level keys: destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style, overview, itinerary, tips
The itinerary array length equals the numeric days value provided in the request
Invalid or missing required input returns HTTP 400 with a JSON error object describing the problem

user_stories.goal: Implement a stable, deterministic itinerary generation contract that automated tests and Docker deployment can rely on
user_stories.benefit: Ensure repeatable outputs for pytest checks and predictable production behaviour
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/itinerary behaviour is deterministic for the same inputs (no unstabilized randomness in outputs)
All activity titles and descriptions in the response are human-readable strings (no raw objects like [object Object])
Activity entries may be objects but include readable fields the frontend can render; estimated costs are numeric values (not strings)
The API never creates or requires an image preview area in responses (image integration is handled separately)

user_stories.goal: Produce varied, non-repeating multi-day itineraries that expand interests into many concrete activities and rotate time-slot items
user_stories.benefit: Tourists experience fresh, themed daily plans rather than repeating generic suggestions
acceptance_criteria: Each declared interest is expanded into multiple distinct concrete activities before assembling the itinerary (e.g., food -> street breakfast, market tasting, signature restaurant, dessert stop, evening snack)
Daily activities rotate by morning, afternoon and evening and use destination, day number, time slot and user interests to vary titles so the same generic fallback is not repeated
Where possible each day has a different theme or planning focus and activities reflect that theme
No single three-activity set is visibly repeated across multiple days

user_stories.goal: Render API results into a web UI using a stable set of IDs and helper functions so automated checks can validate the front-end
user_stories.benefit: Maintainable UI that integrates reliably with the backend contract and test harness
acceptance_criteria: The frontend uses the stable element IDs: plannerForm, destination, days, budget, interests, travel_style, resultsContainer, daysContainer, formMessage, errorMessage
The frontend implements the stable functions: collectFormData, validateFormData, setLoading, showError, renderItinerary, renderDay, renderActivity, formatActivityText
renderActivity and formatActivityText produce readable text from activity objects so the UI never displays raw object serialization like [object Object]
The frontend does not create its own image preview area

user_stories.goal: Verify per-day structure and budget handling in itinerary responses to ensure clarity and correct numeric cost adjustments
user_stories.benefit: Users receive clear daily budgets and cost estimates that match their selected budget level
acceptance_criteria: Each itinerary day object includes keys: day (numeric or string day label), morning, afternoon, evening, and budget_note
Each activity includes an estimated_cost numeric field; values are adjusted by the requested budget level (not textual low/medium/high)
Each day's budget_note explains the day's expected spend in readable text and is consistent with summed estimated_costs for that day
The top-level response includes a tips field with human-readable guidance for the trip
